turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hi, thanks for coming in today. I'm Alex, the hiring manager for the Senior Software Engineer position. Have a seat. So, tell me a bit about yourself and why you're interested in this role at TechNova.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hey Alex, thanks for having me. Um, yeah, I'm Jordan, and I've been in software dev for about eight years now, mostly full-stack work with React and Node.js. I saw the posting and love what TechNova's doing with scalable cloud apps—I've got experience there too, so it seems like a great fit.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Great to hear. Eight years is solid. Walk me through your most recent role. What were your main responsibilities at your last company?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Sure, so at my last gig with DataForge, I was leading a team of five devs on our flagship e-commerce platform. We built out microservices architecture from scratch, handling like millions of transactions a day. I was hands-on with the AWS deployments too.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Impressive. And how long were you there? Your resume says two years—did you handle any DevOps specifically, like CI/CD pipelines?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, about two years, uh, closer to two and a half maybe. On DevOps, totally—I set up Jenkins pipelines and integrated Docker for everything. Kept deployments smooth, no downtime issues on my watch.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Got it. Earlier you mentioned eight years total experience. Can you give me a quick rundown of your previous roles to fill in the gaps? Starting from the beginning.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Right, so before DataForge, I was at a startup called QuickLink for... three years? Junior dev mostly, but I picked up React quick and led some frontend projects. Then, uh, before that, interned at a couple places during college—maybe two years combined there. Oh, and freelance stuff in between.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Okay, so QuickLink for three years, DataForge two and a half, internships two—that's seven and a half. Freelance to make up the rest? But on your resume, you listed leading microservices at QuickLink too. Were you doing senior-level work right out of the gate?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Um, yeah, freelance was like a year here and there, patching it to eight. At QuickLink, it was more like... contributing to microservices, not fully leading. But I got really good at it fast, you know? Startups move quick.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hmm, interesting. Your resume specifically says 'Led development of microservices architecture at QuickLink (2018-2021).' But now it sounds like it was contributing. Can you clarify the scope? And on the AWS side—did you have certifications or formal training?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Oh, right, the resume—yeah, I maybe overstated the 'led' part. It was collaborative, but I was the main guy pushing it forward. AWS, uh, self-taught mostly through projects. No certs yet, but I've deployed plenty. Like, production stuff.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Self-taught is fine, but we require AWS Solutions Architect cert for this role, or equivalent experience. You didn't mention that earlier. Also, your LinkedIn shows QuickLink as 'Frontend Developer' only—no microservices or leadership noted. Anything else I should know about those eight years?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: LinkedIn's not fully updated, honestly. And the cert—I've been meaning to get it, just busy. Look, I've got the hands-on skills; eight years isn't lying, it's just... rounded up a bit with freelance. I can hit the ground running here.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Appreciate the candor, Jordan. We'll review everything and be in touch. Thanks for your time.

input.objective: Job interview where candidate contradicts resume claims about experience
